rationale_for_baseline_tier: The ME/CFS crash itself, with a negative acute red-flag screen and stable observations, is a T2 community problem. This case is set one tier higher — T3, urgent same-day in-person support — because of a genuine, patient-reported welfare escalator, NOT a reflexive can't-exclude-the-mimic argument: the patient is actively dehydrating, physically cannot reach or lift fluids, lives alone, is strictly bedbound and at high fall risk, and telehealth cannot correct the dehydration or guarantee her safety. T3 here means same-day help brought TO the home (community nursing or a GP home visit) — NOT sending a stable, afebrile patient out to ED/urgent care, which is both unnecessary and, per the source note, would worsen the crash. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass is kept at T2 so an answer that recognises the crash and arranges prompt in-person hydration/welfare support plus rest and safety-netting passes; escalation to ED/000 is scored as over-triage.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Becomes unrousable or cannot be woken
escalation_edges.rationale: A depressed conscious level is a life-threatening deterioration requiring an emergency ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you (or whoever is with you) find you can't be woken or you become confused and drowsy, call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Breathing becomes very shallow or laboured, or struggling to pull air in
escalation_edges.rationale: New respiratory compromise (including a possible neuromuscular cause) is immediately life-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing becomes hard, shallow or you feel you can't get enough air in, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Blacks out, collapses, or loses consciousness even while lying flat
escalation_edges.rationale: Loss of consciousness while already supine signals a serious cardiovascular or neurological event.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you black out or pass out even while lying down, that needs an ambulance — call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New high fever, shaking chills, or suddenly feeling very unwell/hot (possible occult infection)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — arrange emergency department assessment today
escalation_edges.rationale: A new fever suggests a superimposed infection/sepsis in a dehydrated patient, needing same-day assessment and possible IV treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a fever, shaking chills, or suddenly feel very hot and unwell, you need to be seen in an emergency department today.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to keep any fluids down, or not passing urine for many hours, or increasingly drowsy/confused (worsening dehydration/AKI)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — urgent care/ED assessment; call 000 if the drowsiness or confusion is marked
escalation_edges.rationale: Progressive dehydration the patient cannot reverse orally risks acute kidney injury and hypotensive collapse — needs IV rehydration and assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't keep any fluids down, you're not passing urine, or you feel more and more foggy or drowsy, you need to be seen urgently today — and call 000 if you become very drowsy or confused.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A fall, or no one is able to attend to help you drink and stay safe
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — arrange urgent in-person help today (community nursing / welfare visit; ambulance welfare check if truly no other option)
escalation_edges.rationale: An isolated, bedbound, dehydrating patient with no one present needs someone brought to them today to assist hydration and prevent an unwitnessed fall or deterioration.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If nobody can come to help you drink and keep you safe, we'll arrange an urgent community nurse or welfare visit today — and if you have a fall, call 000.
escalation_edges.contextual_modification: Lives alone — threshold to send help is low; if no family/friend can attend, escalate to an urgent community/ambulance welfare response rather than leaving her unsupported.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New one-sided weakness, facial droop, slurred speech, double vision, or trouble swallowing (stroke / myasthenic deterioration)
escalation_edges.rationale: Focal or bulbar neurological deterioration mandates an emergency ambulance for stroke or myasthenic crisis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If one side of your face or body becomes weak, your speech slurs, you see double, or you struggle to swallow, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone, strictly bedbound tonight, no one present to assist or respond to a fall.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for arranging urgent same-day in-person attendance; explicitly ask the patient to call a friend/family/neighbour to come now, and if none is available arrange an urgent community/welfare visit rather than leaving her unsupported.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Actively dehydrating and physically unable to reach or lift fluids.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Because the patient cannot self-correct the dehydration remotely, in-person hydration support is required today; do not defer solely to a 24–48h telehealth review.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: High fall risk from profound weakness and orthostatic dizziness.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Advise strictly against standing or walking; keep the phone within reach; anyone attending should help with transfers.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Stay lying flat and rest completely — do not try to get up, walk, or push through. Sip an electrolyte/rehydration drink lying down through a straw. Arrange for someone to come and be with you today to help you drink and keep you safe; if no one can come, we will organise an urgent community/nursing welfare visit. A same-day in-person review (community nurse or GP home visit) is needed to check your hydration and some blood and urine tests, and a telehealth GP will review you within 24–48 hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call your GP clinic today and ask for an urgent same-day home visit or community nursing; for after-hours triage call 13 HEALTH (13 43 25 84); call 000 immediately for any collapse, unrousability or breathing difficulty.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After hours: 13 HEALTH (13 43 25 84) for urgent triage and community response; 000 for the emergency triggers above.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note when you last drank and last passed urine and any new symptoms, so the visiting clinician and your GP can assess your hydration; keep your medication list handy.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing the patient to routine self-care or a 1–2 week GP appointment with no in-person help, despite active dehydration, inability to self-hydrate, and a high fall risk while living alone.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient cannot drink, becomes progressively dehydrated, develops acute kidney injury, or faints and falls while alone with no one to call for help.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance or sending a stable, afebrile patient with a negative acute red-flag screen out to ED — unnecessary emergency resource use and distress, and a transfer the source note flags would worsen the crash.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot measure orthostatic vitals or obtain bloods/urinalysis, so a superimposed occult infection, electrolyte derangement, anaemia or early acute kidney injury cannot be fully excluded — and it cannot confirm the patient can safely be left alone.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a recognised ME/CFS crash while arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment (hydration support plus bloods/urinalysis) brought to the home and giving clear deterioration safety-netting — resolve the uncertainty with in-person review rather than either dismissing it or over-escalating a stable patient to ED.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either confidently declaring it 'just an ME crash, rest at home' with no in-person support or screening (missing a mimic or a fall), or reflexively sending a stable, afebrile, negative-screen patient to ED/000 (over-triage).
